problem.title: GenZ-ICP profile trade-off on the NCLT 2013-01-10 window
problem.question: Which GenZ-ICP profile should be the default on NCLT, and does the adaptive hybrid help over the KISS-style baseline?

variants.label: Default profile
variants.intent: Default GenZ-ICP adaptive point-to-plane / point-to-point hybrid.
variants.note: Adaptive point-to-plane / point-to-point hybrid odometry (no GT seed; anchor matches first GT pose).
variants.readability_note: Uses the default CLI surface only.
variants.extensibility_note: No extra profile knobs beyond the stable core contract.
variants.decision_reason: Best combined benchmark score on the shared dataset and interface.

variants.label: Fast profile
variants.intent: Larger voxels / fewer points / fewer iterations for speed.
variants.note: Adaptive point-to-plane / point-to-point hybrid odometry (no GT seed; anchor matches first GT pose).
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: Dense profile
variants.intent: Smaller voxels / more points / more iterations for accuracy.
variants.note: Adaptive point-to-plane / point-to-point hybrid odometry (no GT seed; anchor matches first GT pose).
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.
